_documentation.description: Reference template for the self-service registration flow. This flow listens to the 'Register Your Form' Microsoft Form, posts the raw registration response to /api/register-form, and on success uses HTTP with Microsoft Entra ID to post flow_create_body to the Flow API. This is NOT an importable flow definition.
related_files: docs/setup-guide.md - deployment and registration flow runbook
docs/admin-guide.md - PHI review and activation workflow
notes: The registration form asks three questions: form share link, short description/name, and whether the form collects patient information.
The HTTP action should be renamed to RegisterForm so the flow_create_body expression can be copied exactly.
On success, the flow posts body('RegisterForm')?['flow_create_body'] to the Flow API to create the per-form response-processing flow.
Non-PHI forms are stored as active. PHI forms are stored as pending_review; the per-form flow can still be created, but /api/process-response returns 403 until IT activates the form.
Current implementation does not send Teams review notifications or clinician confirmation emails from this flow.

Before using this flow, set FUNCTION_APP_KEY, FORMS_CONNECTION_NAME, OUTLOOK_CONNECTION_NAME, and ALERT_EMAIL on the Function App so generated flows contain the right connection references and alert target.

flow_name: Forms to Fabric: Register New Form

trigger.type: When a new response is submitted

actions.name: Get_response_details
actions._comment: Retrieves the full registration-form response so the Azure Function can parse the three answers from raw_response.

actions.name: RegisterForm
actions._comment: Posts the registration form response to the Azure Function. The function extracts form_url, description, and has_phi from raw_response and returns flow_create_body.

actions.name: Create_Per_Form_Flow
actions.display_name: Invoke an HTTP request - Flow API create flow
actions._comment: Use the HTTP with Microsoft Entra ID connector. Resource URI is https://service.flow.microsoft.com and Base URL is https://api.flow.microsoft.com.

inputs.subject: Forms to Fabric - Registration error
inputs.body:
Form registration failed

Submitted form link: @{body('Get_response_details')}

HTTP Status Code: @{outputs('RegisterForm')?['statusCode']}

Error Details:

@{body('RegisterForm')}

Timestamp: @{utcNow()}

Review the registration flow run history and register the form manually if needed.

AZURE_FUNCTION_BASE_URL.description: Base URL of the Azure Function App (for example: https://<function-app-name>.azurewebsites.net). The flow appends /api/register-form.

AZURE_FUNCTION_KEY.description: Current function or host key used by the registration flow's HTTP POST action.

REGISTRATION_FORM_ID.description: Microsoft Form ID for the 'Register Your Form for Analytics' intake form.

FLOW_ENVIRONMENT_ID.description: Power Automate environment ID used by the Flow API POST, typically Default-<tenant-id>.

ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL.description: Monitored mailbox or distribution list that receives registration failure alerts.

setup_instructions: 1. Create the registration Microsoft Form using docs/registration-form-template.md.
2. Set FUNCTION_APP_KEY, FORMS_CONNECTION_NAME, OUTLOOK_CONNECTION_NAME, and ALERT_EMAIL on the Function App before clinicians start registering forms.
3. Create a new Automated Cloud Flow in Power Automate and choose the registration form in the trigger.
5. Add an HTTP action to POST to <function-app>/api/register-form, and rename that action to RegisterForm.
6. Use the raw_response body shape shown in this template or generate it with pwsh scripts/Generate-FlowBody.ps1 -Registration.
7. Add a condition that checks for status code 200.
8. In the success branch, add 'Invoke an HTTP request' with HTTP with Microsoft Entra ID and set the body to body('RegisterForm')?['flow_create_body'].
9. In the error branch, add an Outlook email action for admin alerting.
10. Save the flow and test with both a non-PHI and PHI registration.
